featuresSearchApiPage.title: Search API
featuresSearchApiPage.description: A simple, powerful REST API for lightning-fast search over your data. Tenant-isolated, rate-limited, and observable.

featuresSearchApi.title: The API your team deserves
featuresSearchApi.subtitle: 15 REST endpoints. OpenAPI 3.1 spec. Browser SDK. No search cluster to manage.

endpoints.title: 15 public endpoints
endpoints.description: Full CRUD over indexes, documents, and API keys. Plus search, multi-search, analytics, and OpenAPI spec at /api/v1/openapi.json.

openApi.title: OpenAPI 3.1 spec
openApi.description: Machine-readable spec ships with every deployment. Generate clients in any language. Type-safe from day one.

browserSdk.title: Typed browser SDK
browserSdk.description: NPM package. Single search, multi-search (federated), facets. Under 50ms p99. Works in any framework.

authentication.title: API key authentication
authentication.description: Bearer token auth. Keys hashed at rest. Scoped tokens for browser use. Origin allow-list per key.

tenancy.title: Built-in multi-tenancy
tenancy.description: Every request is isolated to your organization. Cross-org reads are architecturally impossible — not just policy.

observability.title: Observability built in
observability.description: Every search is recorded with query, result count, latency, and user agent. Analytics and top-queries in the dashboard.

featuresAnalyticsPage.title: Search analytics
featuresAnalyticsPage.description: Track top queries, zero-result searches, and click-through rates. AACsearch analytics surface user intent automatically — no extra instrumentation required.

featuresAnalytics.title: Turn searches into insights
featuresAnalytics.subtitle: Every query is an implicit user intent signal. AACsearch captures, aggregates, and surfaces them automatically.

topQueries.title: Top queries
topQueries.description: See the most frequent search terms ranked by volume. Spot trends, emerging demand, and seasonal patterns.

zeroResults.title: Zero-results tracking
zeroResults.description: Every search that returns nothing is flagged. Build synonyms, add missing content, or surface alternatives.

clickThrough.title: Click-through rate
clickThrough.description: Track which results users click per query. Identify ranking problems before they hurt conversion.

recentActivity.title: Recent activity feed
recentActivity.description: Live feed of search events across your org. Filter by index, date range, or result count.

usageSummary.title: Usage summary
usageSummary.description: Monthly search-unit consumption, broken down by index. Forecast costs and plan capacity before hitting limits.

widgetEvents.title: Widget event tracking
widgetEvents.description: widget_open, search_query, result_click, filter_used — all captured automatically from the embeddable widget.

featuresAnalytics.codeSubtitle: The widget sends search events via a single POST. Nothing to instrument.

featuresWidgetPage.title: Embeddable search widget
featuresWidgetPage.description: A 14KB JavaScript widget with Shadow DOM isolation. Built-in analytics, 5 locales, zero framework dependency — drop instant search into any storefront in under an hour.

featuresWidget.title: Search that drops into any page
featuresWidget.subtitle: Vanilla JS, no framework dependency, served from your AACsearch subdomain. Works on PrestaShop, Bitrix, WordPress, and custom storefronts.

shadowDom.title: Shadow DOM isolation
shadowDom.description: Widget styles never leak into your page. Your page styles never break the widget. Zero CSS conflicts.

noFramework.title: No framework required
noFramework.description: Pure JavaScript IIFE. Works alongside React, Vue, plain HTML, or any CMS. No build step, no npm, no bundler.

i18n.title: 5-locale built-in
i18n.description: English, German, Spanish, French, and Russian translations built into the widget bundle. Auto-detects locale.

analytics.title: Analytics out of the box
analytics.description: Tracks widget_open, search_query, zero_results, result_click, and filter_used automatically. No extra code.

config.title: Data-attribute config
config.description: Configure via data-* attributes on the script tag. No JavaScript required. Token, placeholder, locale — all attributes.

performance.title: 14KB gzipped
performance.description: IIFE + ESM dual build. Fast initial load, instant search results. No async chunks, no dynamic imports.

featuresWidget.codeSubtitle: Copy. Paste. Replace the token. Instant search is live.

featuresKnowledgePage.title: Knowledge base & RAG
featuresKnowledgePage.description: Index your documents and answer questions over them with AI. Chunking, retrieval, and GraphRAG — included.

featuresKnowledge.title: Q&A over your documents
featuresKnowledge.subtitle: Upload PDFs, DOCX, and text files. Ask questions in natural language. Get cited answers backed by your content.

ingestion.title: Multi-format ingestion
ingestion.description: PDF, DOCX, TXT, and Markdown. Upload via the dashboard or the ingest-file API. Status tracked per ingestion job.

chunking.title: Smart chunking
chunking.description: Documents are split into semantic chunks with overlap. Chunk size and strategy configurable per knowledge space.

retrieval.title: Semantic retrieval
retrieval.description: OpenAI embeddings for semantic similarity. BM25 for keyword precision. Hybrid scoring for best-of-both results.

graphRag.title: GraphRAG
graphRag.description: Entity extraction builds a knowledge graph over your documents. Multi-hop reasoning surfaces connections plain search misses.

citations.title: Cited answers
citations.description: Every answer includes source references with document name, chunk position, and relevance score.

multiTenant.title: Per-tenant spaces
multiTenant.description: Each org has isolated knowledge spaces. Users only see their own data. No cross-tenant information leakage.

featuresConnectorsPage.title: CMS connectors
featuresConnectorsPage.description: Native PHP modules for PrestaShop 8.x and 1C-Bitrix. 7-endpoint REST Connector API for custom integrations. Full and delta sync with automatic retries on failure.

featuresConnectors.title: Connect your data source
featuresConnectors.subtitle: Pre-built modules handle the sync loop. The 7-endpoint Connector API lets you write a connector in any language.

prestashop.title: PrestaShop 8.x
prestashop.description: PHP module. Event hooks on product save, update, and delete. Full catalog export with one click.

bitrix.title: 1C-Bitrix
bitrix.description: Native Bitrix module. Background agent for scheduled sync. Event handlers for real-time updates.

connectorApi.title: Connector API
connectorApi.description: 7 endpoints: handshake, full sync, delta sync, delete, diagnostics, sync status, heartbeat. Build your own in any language.

fullSync.title: Full & delta sync
fullSync.description: Full sync re-indexes the entire catalog. Delta sync processes only changed records. Schedule or trigger on demand.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics endpoint
diagnostics.description: Test the connection, check index state, and verify token permissions without touching production data.

syncJobs.title: Sync job history
syncJobs.description: Every sync run is logged with start time, document count, errors, and duration. Retry failed batches from the dashboard.

featuresConnectors.codeSubtitle: Send only changed documents. The API handles deduplication and retries.

featuresScopedTokensPage.title: Scoped search tokens
featuresScopedTokensPage.description: HMAC-signed tokens that narrow permissions to a specific index, user, TTL, and filter — never widen them.

featuresScopedTokens.title: Fine-grained access control
featuresScopedTokens.subtitle: Issue a token server-side, ship it to the browser. It expires, it is locked to one index, and it can only AND-narrow the result set.

hmacSigning.title: HMAC-signed
hmacSigning.description: Tokens are signed with your secret. Tampering or replay is cryptographically detectable.

ttl.title: TTL-bound
ttl.description: Every scoped token carries an expiry. Short-lived tokens for browser sessions; longer TTLs for server-to-server.

filters.title: AND-combined filters
filters.description: A token can carry a mandatory filter (e.g. userId = 42). The server AND-combines it with the caller's filter — never OR.

originLock.title: Origin locking
originLock.description: Pin a token to specific origins. A token exported in JavaScript can only be used from your domain.

indexScope.title: Index-scoped
indexScope.description: Each token is bound to a single index. Cross-index queries require a separate token per index.

serverSide.title: Server-side issuance
serverSide.description: Tokens are issued by your backend via a single API call. The browser never sees your admin API key.

featuresMultiSearchPage.title: Multi-search & federated queries
featuresMultiSearchPage.description: Run multiple searches across different indexes in one HTTP request. Build cross-collection autocomplete under 50ms.

featuresMultiSearch.title: One request, many results
featuresMultiSearch.subtitle: Federated search across indexes, collections, and data types — without N round-trips from the browser.

federation.title: Cross-index federation
federation.description: Query products, categories, and blog posts in one call. Merge and rank results client-side or let the API do it.

autocomplete.title: Autocomplete
autocomplete.description: Type-ahead suggestions across multiple collections in one request. Under 50ms p99 for instant feel.

batchedRequests.title: Batched requests
batchedRequests.description: Up to 50 searches per multi-search request. Each with independent filters, sort, and facets.

parallelExecution.title: Parallel execution
parallelExecution.description: All searches in a multi-search call execute in parallel on the server. Total latency = slowest single query.

independentFacets.title: Independent facets
independentFacets.description: Each search in the batch returns its own facet counts. Build multi-panel faceted UIs without extra requests.

singleToken.title: Single token
singleToken.description: One scoped token authorizes all searches in the batch. Tenant isolation is enforced per query inside the batch.

featuresReindexPage.title: Zero-downtime reindex
featuresReindexPage.description: Rebuild your index against a new schema while live traffic keeps hitting the old one. Cut over atomically.

featuresReindex.title: Schema changes without downtime
featuresReindex.subtitle: Version your indexes. Rebuild in the background. Swap the alias when ready. Rollback in one command.

versioning.title: Versioned indexes
versioning.description: Every index has a version number (e.g. org_products_v3). Aliases point to the active version transparently.

aliasSwap.title: Atomic alias swap
aliasSwap.description: Reindex into a new version while traffic hits the old. Swap the alias in a single atomic operation — zero dropped requests.

backgroundRebuild.title: Background rebuild
backgroundRebuild.description: Reindex runs out of band. Your API continues serving search from the current version throughout.

rollback.title: One-command rollback
rollback.description: If the new version has issues, point the alias back to the previous version. Traffic shifts instantly.

schemaChanges.title: Schema evolution
schemaChanges.description: Add fields, change types, update facet settings — all handled by creating a new version, not mutating the live index.

verification.title: Built-in verification
verification.description: After reindex, run a verification query before the alias swap. Catch missing documents before users notice.

featuresRateLimitingPage.title: Rate limiting & quota
featuresRateLimitingPage.description: Per-key per-minute throttling and per-org monthly quota. Both visible in the dashboard before you hit them.

featuresRateLimiting.title: Protect your budget and your service
featuresRateLimiting.subtitle: Configurable limits at every layer. You control what happens when limits are reached.

perKeyThrottling.title: Per-key throttling
perKeyThrottling.description: Set requests-per-minute per API key. Burst traffic from one key does not affect others on your account.

orgQuota.title: Monthly org quota
orgQuota.description: Hard limit on search-units per month per organization. Configurable per plan. Dashboard shows usage in real time.

graceful429.title: Graceful 429 responses
graceful429.description: Rate-limited requests return HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header. The browser SDK handles retries automatically.

dashboardVisibility.title: Dashboard visibility
dashboardVisibility.description: Current usage, limit, and projected end-of-month consumption visible at a glance. No surprise overages.

planBased.title: Plan-based limits
planBased.description: Free, Pro, and Enterprise plans carry different quotas and rate limits. Upgrade without touching your integration.

gracePeriod.title: Cancellation grace period
gracePeriod.description: 7-day read-only grace on cancellation. Search keeps working while you migrate. Writes are blocked after cancellation.

useCasesEcommercePage.title: Search for e-commerce
useCasesEcommercePage.description: Faceted filters, synonym expansion, zero-results handling, and real-time analytics — built for product catalogs.

useCasesEcommerce.title: Product search that converts

compare.title: The honest comparison
compare.subtitle: We are not the right choice for every team. Here is a frank breakdown of where AACsearch wins and where it does not.

vsAlgolia.title: AACsearch vs Algolia
vsAlgolia.description: Algolia is polished and has excellent docs. AACsearch is more affordable and built for multi-tenant teams. If you are spending $500+/mo on Algolia, AACsearch will cut that significantly.

vsElasticsearch.title: AACsearch vs Elasticsearch
vsElasticsearch.description: Elasticsearch is infinitely flexible and infinitely complex. AACsearch is opinionated and simple. If you need a SIEM or log analytics, use Elastic. If you need product search, use us.

vsMeilisearch.title: AACsearch vs Meilisearch
vsMeilisearch.description: Meilisearch is an excellent open-source engine. AACsearch adds the hosted layer: scoped tokens, multi-tenancy, analytics, and a control plane.

vsTypesense.title: AACsearch vs Typesense Cloud
vsTypesense.description: AACsearch is built on Typesense. We add multi-tenancy, API key management, scoped tokens, and a SaaS control plane on top of the engine.

vsCustom.title: AACsearch vs DIY
vsCustom.description: Building your own hosted search means managing nodes, upgrades, backups, and monitoring. AACsearch does that so you can ship features.

whenNotUs.title: When to use something else
whenNotUs.description: Log analytics → Elasticsearch. Full-text enterprise search with compliance → Coveo. Simple autocomplete on 1K records → don't bother with any service.

compareAlgoliaPage.title: AACsearch vs Algolia: 5x Cheaper Search with Zero Lock-In (2026)
compareAlgoliaPage.description: Detailed AACsearch vs Algolia comparison. AACsearch delivers the same search quality at 1/5th the cost with instant deployment, built-in analytics, Typesense-powered relevance, and no vendor lock-in.

compareAlgolia.title: AACsearch vs Algolia — The Honest Comparison
compareAlgolia.subtitle: Algolia is the market leader with excellent DX. AACsearch is more affordable and adds multi-tenant isolation, a search widget, and CMS connectors — all included.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.description: Algolia charges per search operation and per record stored, with minimums. AACsearch charges flat per-index pricing with unlimited operations. At 100k docs + 500k searches/mo: Algolia ~$499/mo, AACsearch $99/mo.

performance.title: Performance
performance.description: AACsearch is powered by Typesense — the fastest open-source search engine with sub-10ms query latency. Algolia offers comparable speed but costs significantly more per query.

multiTenancy.title: Multi-tenancy
multiTenancy.description: Algolia uses per-index API keys with user tokens. AACsearch uses HMAC scoped tokens with organization-level isolation — built-in, no middleware needed.

security.title: Security & Lock-in
security.description: Algolia uses a proprietary backend — your data is locked in. AACsearch is powered by Typesense (open-source). Export your data anytime and self-host with zero migration cost.

migration.title: Migration
migration.description: The AACsearch REST API is structurally similar to Algolia's. A migration script can export your Algolia data and import it into AACsearch in minutes.

verdict.title: When to choose AACsearch
verdict.description: If you are spending $200+/mo on Algolia, or if multi-tenancy isolation and predictable pricing matter for your business, AACsearch is a clear upgrade.

compareElasticsearch.title: AACsearch vs Elasticsearch — The Honest Comparison
compareElasticsearch.subtitle: Elasticsearch is a powerful general-purpose data platform. AACsearch is a purpose-built search API. Different tools for different jobs — here is when each makes sense.

complexity.title: Operational complexity
complexity.description: Elasticsearch requires cluster management, JVM tuning, shard optimization, and ongoing maintenance. AACsearch is a hosted API — zero-ops, no infrastructure to manage.

latency.title: Search latency
latency.description: Elasticsearch p99 search latency is typically 100-400ms depending on cluster size and query complexity. AACsearch targets under 50ms p99 for product and document search workloads.

useCase.title: Use case fit
useCase.description: Elasticsearch excels at log analytics, observability pipelines, full-text enterprise search, and complex aggregations. AACsearch excels at product search, help center search, and SaaS application search.

cost.title: Cost
cost.description: Elasticsearch Cloud is expensive for pure search workloads — you pay for cluster nodes you don't need. AACsearch pricing is based on search-units — pay only for what you use.

schema.title: Schema management
schema.description: Elasticsearch mapping changes are complex and often require reindexing with downtime. AACsearch has zero-downtime alias-swap reindex — schema changes without any interruption.

verdict.title: When to choose AACsearch
verdict.description: If your use case is product search, help center, or SaaS full-text search — and you do not need log analytics or complex aggregations — AACsearch is the simpler, cheaper, faster option.

compareElasticsearch.codeSubtitle: AACsearch is a managed search API. Elasticsearch requires cluster provisioning, JVM tuning, and ongoing maintenance.

radiusSearch.title: Radius queries
radiusSearch.description: Return results within N km or miles of a point. Configurable unit, precision, and maximum result count.

boundingBox.title: Bounding-box search
boundingBox.description: Filter to a geographic rectangle using NE and SW coordinates. Useful for map-viewport search in web and mobile apps.

distanceSorting.title: Distance sorting

multipleLocations.title: Multiple location fields
multipleLocations.description: Index multiple lat/lng fields per document — e.g. pickup and delivery locations — and query against either or both.

performance.title: Sub-50ms latency

featuresInstantSearchPage.title: Instant Search

featuresInstantSearch.title: Search that keeps up
featuresInstantSearch.subtitle: AACsearch is designed for as-you-type use cases. The hosted widget and browser SDK handle debouncing, prefetching, and caching for you.

asYouType.title: As-you-type results
asYouType.description: Results update on every keystroke. Prefix matching and typo tolerance mean relevant hits appear from the first character.

debouncing.title: Built-in debouncing
debouncing.description: The widget and SDK debounce input automatically. Configurable delay (default 200ms) prevents unnecessary API calls.

caching.title: Client-side caching
caching.description: Repeated queries return from the browser cache instantly. Cache is keyed by query + filters + locale.

prefetching.title: Query prefetching
prefetching.description: Prefetch popular queries on page load so the first keystroke resolves from cache instead of a network request.

latency.title: Sub-50ms API latency
latency.description: AACsearch P99 latency is under 50ms. Combined with edge caching, end-to-end time stays well under 100ms.

widget.title: Drop-in widget
widget.description: The AACsearch widget ships with instant-search UI out of the box. One script tag, zero configuration required.

featuresInstantSearch.codeSubtitle: Drop the widget into any HTML page. No npm, no bundler, no framework.

featuresFacetedSearchPage.title: Faceted Search & Navigation
featuresFacetedSearchPage.description: Dynamic facets with real-time counts. Give users the navigation controls that move them from query to result in one click.

featuresFacetedSearch.title: Every filter, every click
featuresFacetedSearch.subtitle: AACsearch computes facet counts server-side on every query. No client-side post-filtering, no stale counts, no separate aggregation pass.

dynamicFacets.title: Dynamic facets
dynamicFacets.description: Define which fields are facetable in your collection schema. Counts are recomputed on every query with sub-millisecond overhead.

countFacets.title: Accurate counts
countFacets.description: Facet counts reflect the current query context — apply a price filter and category counts update immediately.

rangeFacets.title: Range facets
rangeFacets.description: Numeric range facets for price, rating, year, and custom fields. Configurable bucket sizes and display formats.

nestedFacets.title: Nested category facets
nestedFacets.description: Multi-level category trees rendered as hierarchical facets. Breadcrumb navigation generated automatically.

performance.title: No aggregation overhead
performance.description: Facet computation is co-located with search execution. One API call returns results and facet counts together.

mobile.title: Mobile-first facets

featuresTypoTolerancePage.title: Typo Tolerance

featuresTypoTolerance.title: Search that reads your mind
featuresTypoTolerance.subtitle: AACsearch applies typo tolerance per-query. Zero config needed — it works well out of the box and is tunable when required.

editDistance.title: Configurable edit distance
editDistance.description: Levenshtein distance is configurable per token. Default: 1 typo for words with 5+ chars, 2 typos for words with 8+ chars.

prefixMatch.title: Prefix matching
prefixMatch.description: Incomplete words match documents that start with the typed prefix. Powers as-you-type autocomplete without exact-match requirements.

splitWords.title: Split-word correction
splitWords.description: 'macbook pro' matches 'macbookpro' and vice versa. Split-word detection catches missed spaces and compound words.

customDictionary.title: Custom dictionary
customDictionary.description: Whitelist brand names, product codes, and technical terms that should never be corrected by the typo-tolerance engine.

performance.title: Zero-latency overhead
performance.description: Typo tolerance is implemented inside AACsearch's search core. No extra round-trip, no external spell-check service.

accuracy.title: High precision
accuracy.description: Short queries with many short words skip typo tolerance to avoid false positives. Configurable minimum token length.

featuresSynonymsPage.title: Synonyms Management

featuresSynonyms.title: Every word they use, every document you have
featuresSynonyms.subtitle: Synonyms bridge the gap between how users describe things and how your catalogue names them. Manage rules in the dashboard, no redeploy needed.

oneWay.title: One-way synonyms
oneWay.description: Map 'sofa to couch' without the reverse. Use one-way rules when your catalogue uses the formal term but users type the informal one.

multiWay.title: Multi-way synonyms
multiWay.description: List equivalent terms: 'jumper, sweater, pullover, knitwear'. Any term matches documents containing any other.

csvImport.title: CSV import
csvImport.description: Import synonym pairs in bulk from a CSV file. Paste your existing synonym list and it is applied instantly.

realTimeUpdate.title: No-downtime updates
realTimeUpdate.description: Add or remove synonym rules without reindexing. Changes take effect on the next query, not the next deployment.

scope.title: Per-collection scope
scope.description: Different synonym libraries per collection. Your products index and your docs index can have independent synonym rules.

analytics.title: Synonym effectiveness
analytics.description: See which synonym rules fire most often and which queries still return zero results despite synonym expansion.

featuresRelevanceTuningPage.title: Relevance Tuning

featuresRelevanceTuning.title: Results that match intent
featuresRelevanceTuning.subtitle: AACsearch separates retrieval from ranking. Tune relevance in the dashboard — no schema migration, no reindex, no redeploy.

fieldWeighting.title: Field weights
fieldWeighting.description: Assign relative importance to title, description, tags, and custom fields. A title match outweighs a description match by default.

boostRules.title: Boost rules
boostRules.description: Boost documents where in_stock is true or rating is 4.5+. Conditional boosts express merchandising logic declaratively.

pinnedResults.title: Pinned results
pinnedResults.description: Fix specific documents to position 1, 2, or 3 for a given query. Used for sponsored results, featured products, and editorial picks.

merchandising.title: Merchandising presets

scoring.title: Custom scoring

analytics.title: Relevance analytics
analytics.description: Measure CTR and zero-result rate before and after a tuning change. Roll back in one click if metrics regress.

featuresCurationsPage.title: Curations & Pinning

featuresCurations.title: Manual control, automatic delivery

pinned.title: Pinned results
pinned.description: Place specific documents at exact positions for a query. Sponsored items, featured products, or editorial picks stay where you put them.

excluded.title: Excluded results
excluded.description: Remove specific documents from a query result set entirely. Useful for out-of-season products or content under legal review.

promoted.title: Promoted documents
promoted.description: Elevate a document to the first page without assigning a fixed position. Promoted results compete at a higher score than organic results.

triggerKeywords.title: Query triggers
triggerKeywords.description: Curation rules activate on exact queries, prefix matches, or synonym matches. One rule can cover hundreds of query variants.

schedule.title: Scheduled curations
schedule.description: Set a start and end date on any curation rule. Promotional placements activate and deactivate automatically without manual intervention.

analytics.title: Curation analytics

featuresStopwordsPage.title: Stopwords

featuresStopwords.title: Signal over noise
featuresStopwords.subtitle: Stopwords remove words that add no search signal so index matching focuses on meaningful terms.

customStopwords.title: Custom stopword lists
customStopwords.description: Define your own stopword lists in addition to built-in language-specific defaults. Domain-specific noise words removed instantly.

perCollection.title: Per-collection configuration
perCollection.description: Different stopword lists per collection. Your products index and knowledge base can have independent noise-word sets.

languageAware.title: Language-aware defaults
languageAware.description: Built-in stopword lists for English, German, French, Spanish, and Russian. Override or extend per collection.

analytics.title: Stopword analytics
analytics.description: Identify which removed stopwords correlate with zero-result queries. Fine-tune your list based on real user data.

realTime.title: No-downtime updates
realTime.description: Add or remove stopwords without reindexing. Changes take effect immediately on the next query.

performance.title: Zero query overhead
performance.description: Stopword filtering runs at query parse time, before index lookup. No measurable latency impact.

sdk.title: TypeScript SDK
sdk.description: Install the AACsearch npm package. Full TypeScript types, zero external dependencies, 8KB gzipped. Works in any JS runtime.

serverComponents.title: React Server Components
serverComponents.description: Call the AACsearch API directly from Server Components. No client bundle cost, no hydration overhead for search-result pages.

analytics.title: Edge-compatible analytics
analytics.description: Track search events from the Vercel Edge Network. No cold starts, no timeout risk for lightweight analytics calls.

sdk.title: React SDK

components.title: Headless components

ssr.title: SSR & hydration
ssr.description: Pre-fetch search results on the server and hydrate on the client without a flash of empty content.

typescript.title: Full TypeScript support

analytics.title: Event tracking

compareOpenSearchPage.title: AACsearch vs OpenSearch
compareOpenSearchPage.description: AACsearch is a focused, fast search API. OpenSearch is a full analytics platform. Here is when each makes sense.

compareOpenSearch.title: Focused search vs. analytics platform
compareOpenSearch.subtitle: OpenSearch is powerful but complex. AACsearch is purpose-built for application search — simpler schema, faster setup, lower latency.

complexity.title: Days, not weeks
complexity.description: AACsearch indexes your first collection in an afternoon. OpenSearch cluster setup, mapping design, and ILM policies take a team and weeks.

performance.title: Sub-50ms vs. 100-500ms
performance.description: AACsearch P99 latency is under 50ms. OpenSearch query latency typically ranges 100-500ms on equivalent hardware.

schema.title: Schema simplicity
schema.description: AACsearch collections have a flat, typed schema. OpenSearch mapping design with nested objects and dynamic templates is a specialty skill.

multiTenancy.title: Native multi-tenancy
multiTenancy.description: AACsearch org-scoped keys and scoped tokens are first-class features. OpenSearch multi-tenancy requires index-per-tenant or security plugins.

analytics.title: Search-specific analytics
analytics.description: AACsearch provides query logs, zero-result rates, and CTR — built for product teams. OpenSearch analytics targets infrastructure monitoring.

pricing.title: Predictable cost
pricing.description: AACsearch charges per search-unit. OpenSearch on AWS costs depend on instance type, storage I/O, and data transfer — difficult to forecast.

compareSolrPage.title: AACsearch vs Apache Solr
compareSolrPage.description: Solr is battle-tested but requires specialists. AACsearch gives you the same reliability with a modern API and zero operations overhead.

compareSolr.title: Modern API vs. legacy complexity
compareSolr.subtitle: Solr has powered enterprise search for 20 years. AACsearch gives a junior developer the same capabilities with a one-afternoon setup.

modernApi.title: REST API vs. Solr query DSL
modernApi.description: AACsearch uses a clean JSON REST API. Solr queries use its own DSL — q, fq, fl, facet.field — with a steep learning curve.

setup.title: Minutes vs. days
setup.description: AACsearch setup: create an index, ingest documents, call the API. Solr requires Java, ZooKeeper (for SolrCloud), schema design, and core management.

performance.title: Faster out of the box
performance.description: AACsearch returns results in under 50ms with no tuning. Solr performance requires expert configuration: cache sizing and JVM tuning.

multiTenancy.title: Built-in multi-tenancy
multiTenancy.description: AACsearch isolates tenants at the API key level. Solr multi-tenancy means separate cores or collections with manual access control.

analyticsBuiltin.title: Built-in analytics
analyticsBuiltin.description: AACsearch records every query and provides a dashboard. Solr has no built-in query analytics — you need to parse logs with external tools.

pricing.title: No infrastructure cost
pricing.description: AACsearch is fully managed. Solr means provisioning, operating, and scaling your own cluster — or paying for an expensive managed service.

compareWeaviatePage.title: AACsearch vs Weaviate
compareWeaviatePage.description: Weaviate is a vector database. AACsearch is a full-text plus vector hybrid search API. Here is what you get from each.

compareWeaviate.title: Vector DB vs. hybrid search API
compareWeaviate.subtitle: Both support vectors, but AACsearch adds keyword search, facets, typo tolerance, and a multi-tenant hosting layer that Weaviate lacks.

hybridSearch.title: Keyword + vector in one call
hybridSearch.description: AACsearch hybrid search combines BM25 keyword relevance with vector similarity in one query. Weaviate BM25 support is secondary to its vector focus.

facets.title: Faceted navigation
facets.description: AACsearch computes facet counts on every query — essential for e-commerce and documentation search. Weaviate has no native faceted navigation.

typoTolerance.title: Typo tolerance
typoTolerance.description: AACsearch has built-in fuzzy matching and typo tolerance for end-user queries. Weaviate vector search does not help with misspelled terms.

multiTenancy.title: Native multi-tenancy
multiTenancy.description: AACsearch org-scoped keys and per-tenant analytics are first-class features. Weaviate multi-tenancy requires separate schemas and manual isolation.

pricing.title: Transparent pricing
pricing.description: AACsearch charges per search-unit with a predictable monthly bill. Weaviate cloud pricing is dimension times vector times query — complex to forecast.

widget.title: Drop-in search widget
widget.description: AACsearch ships a 14KB browser widget with instant search, facets, and analytics. Weaviate has no equivalent — you build the UI from scratch.

comparePineconePage.title: AACsearch vs Pinecone
comparePineconePage.description: Pinecone is a pure vector database. AACsearch combines keyword and vector search for applications where both relevance modes matter.

comparePinecone.title: Pure vector vs. full search platform
comparePinecone.subtitle: Pinecone excels at pure vector recall. AACsearch adds the keyword search, facets, and product UX layer that most applications actually need.

keywordSearch.title: Keyword + semantic
keywordSearch.description: AACsearch handles both typo-tolerant keyword search and semantic vector search. Pinecone is vector-only — keyword queries need a separate system.

facets.title: Faceted navigation
facets.description: AACsearch computes facet counts with every query. Pinecone metadata filtering is available but with no facet count computation.

hybridSearch.title: Configurable hybrid weights
hybridSearch.description: AACsearch lets you tune the balance between BM25 and vector similarity per query. Pinecone hybrid search has limited weighting control.

pricing.title: Simpler pricing
pricing.description: AACsearch charges per search-unit. Pinecone charges per vector dimension stored times queries times pod size — complex to model.

performance.title: Search API, not just a vector store
performance.description: AACsearch returns ranked, filtered, highlighted results in under 50ms. Pinecone returns vector IDs and scores — your app assembles the rest.

widget.title: Full search UX included
widget.description: AACsearch includes a hosted widget, analytics dashboard, and SDK. Pinecone is an API — all UX is your responsibility.

glossaryPage.title: Search Glossary
glossaryPage.description: Plain-language definitions of search, indexing, and relevance concepts used across AACsearch documentation and the industry.

glossary.title: The language of search
glossary.subtitle: Search has its own vocabulary. Use this glossary to understand AACsearch documentation and communicate precisely with your team.

searchIndex.title: Search index
searchIndex.description: A data structure optimised for fast text retrieval. AACsearch maintains a search collection per index, with automatic alias management.

facet.title: Facet
facet.description: A filterable attribute returned alongside search results with document counts. Enables guided navigation without additional API calls.

relevance.title: Relevance
relevance.description: How well a document matches a query. AACsearch combines BM25 text relevance, vector similarity, field weights, and custom boost rules.

invertedIndex.title: Inverted index
invertedIndex.description: The core data structure of full-text search. Maps each token to the documents containing it. AACsearch maintains this automatically.

embedding.title: Embedding
embedding.description: A numeric vector representation of text semantics. AACsearch can store and query embeddings for semantic and hybrid search.

searchUnit.title: Search unit
searchUnit.description: AACsearch billing unit. One search unit equals one search query or one document indexed. Used to calculate your monthly plan allocation.

integrationsNextJsSetup.title: Get started in 3 steps
integrationsNextJsSetup.subtitle: No backend changes required. The AACsearch SDK works directly from your Next.js app.

step1.label: Step 1
step1.title: Install the package
step1.description: Run npm install @aacsearch/client in your Next.js project.

step2.label: Step 2
step2.title: Configure your client
step2.description: Create a search client with your API key and index slug. Keep the key server-side or use a scoped read-only token for client components.

step3.label: Step 3
step3.title: Search from any component
step3.description: Call client.search() from a Server Component or use the useSearch hook in a Client Component. Results are typed.

q1.question: Does it work with Next.js 13, 14, and 15?
q1.answer: Yes. The SDK is compatible with Next.js 13, 14, and 15. It works with both the App Router and the Pages Router without any adapters.

q2.question: Can I use it with Vercel Edge Runtime?
q2.answer: Yes. The AACsearch client uses the standard Fetch API and is fully compatible with Vercel Edge Runtime and Cloudflare Workers.

q3.question: How do I protect my API key on the client?
q3.answer: Use a scoped token with read-only permissions for client-side search. Create it from your AACsearch dashboard — the full admin key stays on the server only.

integrationsReactSetup.title: Add search to your React app in 3 steps
integrationsReactSetup.subtitle: Works with React 17, 18, and 19. Framework-agnostic — Next.js, Remix, Vite, and CRA all supported.

step1.label: Step 1
step1.title: Install the package
step1.description: Run npm install @aacsearch/react in your project.

step2.label: Step 2
step2.title: Use the useSearch hook
step2.description: Pass your API key and index slug. The hook returns hits, loading state, and a setQuery function.

step3.label: Step 3
step3.title: Render results
step3.description: Map over hits and display them in your own UI. No required wrapper components — full styling freedom.

q1.question: Which React versions are supported?
q1.answer: React 17, 18, and 19 are supported. The hooks use standard React state and effects — no unstable APIs or experimental features.

q2.question: Does it work with Next.js or Remix?
q2.answer: Yes. The React hooks work in any React environment. For Next.js Server Components, use the base @aacsearch/client package which has no React dependency.

q3.question: Is there a component library or just hooks?
q3.answer: Both. @aacsearch/react includes composable hooks (useSearch, useAutocomplete, useFacets) and unstyled headless components. Bring your own CSS or Tailwind.

integrationsPrestashopSetup.title: Install in 5 minutes
integrationsPrestashopSetup.subtitle: No PHP coding required. The module handles sync automatically after configuration.

step1.label: Step 1
step1.title: Download the module
step1.description: Get aacsearch.zip from your AACsearch dashboard under Connectors → PrestaShop.

step2.label: Step 2
step2.title: Upload to PrestaShop
step2.description: Go to Modules > Module Manager > Upload a module. Select the ZIP and confirm.

step3.label: Step 3
step3.title: Enter your connector token
step3.description: Paste the connector token from your AACsearch dashboard into the module settings and save.

step4.label: Step 4
step4.title: Run full catalog export
step4.description: Click "Full export" in the module admin panel. Your entire product catalog syncs in one batch.

step5.label: Step 5
step5.title: Search is live
step5.description: Products appear in AACsearch immediately. Future saves, updates, and deletes sync automatically via PrestaShop hooks.

q1.question: Which PrestaShop versions are supported?
q1.answer: PrestaShop 8.0 and 8.1 are fully supported. PHP 8.1+ required. Works with all major themes including Hummingbird and Classic.

q2.question: Does the module slow down product saves?
q2.answer: No. Sync is asynchronous via the AACsearch ingest buffer. The module enqueues the update and returns immediately — no blocking calls to Typesense on save.

q3.question: Can I customize which fields are indexed?
q3.answer: Yes. The module exports all product fields by default. You can restrict attributes in the module settings or extend the Exporter class for custom logic.

integrationsBitrixSetup.title: Install in 5 minutes
integrationsBitrixSetup.subtitle: No Bitrix customizations required. The module installs as a standard Bitrix marketplace module.

step1.label: Step 1
step1.title: Download the module
step1.description: Get the aac.search archive from your AACsearch dashboard under Connectors → 1C-Bitrix.

step2.label: Step 2
step2.title: Unpack to /local/modules/
step2.description: Extract the archive to /local/modules/aac.search/ on your Bitrix server. Keep the directory structure intact.

step3.label: Step 3
step3.title: Install from Bitrix admin
step3.description: Go to Bitrix admin → Marketplace → Installed modules. Find "AACsearch" in the list and click Install.

step4.label: Step 4
step4.title: Configure the connector
step4.description: Enter your connector token and iBlock ID in the module settings. Choose the catalog iBlock type and save.

step5.label: Step 5
step5.title: Run initial sync
step5.description: Click "Run full sync" on the Diagnostics tab. All catalog items are indexed. Future changes sync automatically via event handlers.

q1.question: Which Bitrix editions are supported?
q1.answer: Bitrix Site Manager and Business editions are supported. PHP 8.1+ required. Uses standard Bitrix HttpClient — no third-party HTTP libraries needed.

q2.question: How does the background sync agent work?
q2.answer: The module registers a Bitrix agent that runs a periodic full sync for reliability. It catches any catalog changes missed by real-time event handlers, ensuring no products are out of sync.

q3.question: Can I customize which iBlock properties are indexed?
q3.answer: Yes. The module exports all product properties by default. You can limit the fields in the admin settings or extend the ProductExporter class for fully custom field selection.

vsAlgolia.subtitle: Algolia is the market leader with excellent DX. AACsearch is 5x cheaper, built on open-source Typesense, and adds native multi-tenancy with scoped tokens.

pricing.title: Pricing (5x cheaper)
pricing.description: Algolia charges per search operation and per record stored, with tiered minimums that surprise teams at scale. AACsearch charges per search-unit with no minimum beyond the free tier — typically 5x cheaper for 100K+ monthly searches.

performance.title: Performance
performance.description: Both deliver sub-50ms search. AACsearch uses an open-source Typesense engine with consistent p99 latency. Algolia's shared infrastructure can introduce latency spikes during peak traffic.

multiTenancy.title: Multi-tenancy
multiTenancy.description: Algolia uses per-index API keys with optional user tokens for scoping. AACsearch uses HMAC-scoped tokens with mandatory AND-combined filters — tenant isolation is architecturally enforced, not just policy.

security.title: Security
security.description: AACsearch adds per-key origin allow-lists, rate limiting, quota enforcement, and tenant isolation at every query layer. Algolia provides API key management with IP ranges and basic rate limiting. AACsearch covers what Algolia charges extra for.

migration.title: Migration
migration.description: Both APIs are structurally similar. AACsearch provides full Typesense data export and a Typesense-compatible API. Most teams complete the migration in hours using a re-index script. Algolia's proprietary format means vendor lock-in.

openSource.title: Open-source engine
openSource.description: AACsearch runs on Typesense, a fully open-source search engine (GPLv3). You can export your data at any time and run it yourself. Algolia uses a proprietary engine — no data portability if you leave.

ecosystem.title: Ecosystem & integrations
ecosystem.description: Algolia has a mature ecosystem — InstantSearch widgets, connectors, and extensive docs. AACsearch offers a UI widget (React, Vue, vanilla JS), real-time webhook connectors (PrestaShop, Bitrix, Shopify), and a REST API with Browser SDK.

verdict.title: When to choose AACsearch
verdict.description: If you spend $200+/month on Algolia, need native multi-tenancy with scoped tokens, want open-source portability, or prefer predictable flat pricing — AACsearch is the clear choice.

vsAlgolia.codeTitle: Swap the client, keep the query
vsAlgolia.codeSubtitle: Migration is hours, not weeks. AACsearch's Typesense-compatible API mirrors Algolia's query patterns.
